title: Peace agreement between the United States and Iran boosts global markets
summary: Japan celebrates the pact that ends hostilities, as the Nikkei exceeds 69,000 points and the G7 prepares to discuss the new geopolitical landscape.

title: Japan ties with Netherlands in its 2026 World Cup debut
summary: Japan's national team earned a valuable 2-2 draw, with goals from Ogawa and Nakamura, showing great resilience on the field.

title: G7 summit in Évian debates Artificial Intelligence and critical minerals
summary: G7 leaders meet in France to discuss AI regulation, the security of critical mineral supplies, and the recent peace between Iran and the United States.

title: Former Yomiuri Giants manager Shinnosuke Abe avoids prosecution for assault
summary: The Tokyo Public Prosecutors Office decided not to prosecute Abe for the alleged assault on his daughter, and the former manager issued a statement apologizing to his family.

title: Mayor of Shimotsuma, Ibaraki, found dead in a ditch
summary: The body of the newly elected mayor was found in a drainage ditch, and authorities are investigating the possibility of suicide.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi strengthens ties with Italy and the United Kingdom on European tour
summary: During her visit to Europe, Takaichi met with Prime Minister Meloni and Premier Starmer to sign cooperation agreements in advanced technology and defense.

title: Embezzlement of 180 million yen in Red Feather donations in Hokkaido
summary: The organization's secretary-general is accused of diverting approximately 180 million yen from the famous annual donation campaign.

title: Launch of H3 rocket No. 9 scheduled for August 7
summary: JAXA has scheduled the launch of H3 rocket No. 9, the first to carry a commercial satellite after the successful test mission.

title: Two bear incidents keep Japanese authorities on alert
summary: A man was injured by a bear in Gifu despite using spray, while another bear was captured in a trap in Hyogo and returned to its habitat.

title: Opposition lawmaker sparks controversy over comments on Self-Defense Forces recruits
summary: Lawmaker Koichi Koga caused a strong controversy by stating that the Self-Defense Forces recruit those facing economic difficulties, which forced him to retract.

title: Myanmar junta leader makes official visit to China
summary: The Burmese leader meets with President Xi Jinping in Beijing to strengthen trade ties and seek political support amid international isolation.

title: Ukraine suffers massive Russian attacks leaving nine dead
summary: A series of Russian bombardments affected several regions of the country, leaving at least nine fatalities and causing a fire in a historic monastery.

title: Short bets against the yen reach their highest level in nine years
summary: The resurgence of the carry trade has driven bearish bets against the Japanese currency to their highest level since 2016.

title: Trump celebrates his 80th birthday with a martial arts event at the White House
summary: The former US president combined his birthday party with a UFC event, reflecting his close ties to the world of mixed martial arts.

title: 7-Eleven apologizes for overcrowding caused by its smoothie promotion
summary: The half-price smoothie promotion at 7-Eleven stores caused chaos and long lines, forcing the company to issue a public apology.

title: Customer harassment remains a serious problem in Japan's service sector
summary: Despite new regulations, 'customer harassment' continues to affect the service sector, according to a recent report published by The Japan Times.

title: Okinawa residents express fear over relocation of Futenma base
summary: In a trial before the Supreme Court, residents expressed their constant 'anxiety and fear' over the construction project of a new base in Henoko.

title: Bar associations declare the alternative capital bill unconstitutional
summary: Various bar associations argued that the bill to relocate capital functions violates the Constitution of Japan.

title: The deactivation of the AI 'Mythos' was linked to an Amazon report
summary: A complaint from Amazon to the Japanese government triggered the deactivation of the controversial artificial intelligence system, reigniting tensions between the tech sector and the government.

title: Ayako Haraguchi turns 99 and continues to fight for her innocence in the 'Osaki Case'
summary: The nonagenarian, who claims her innocence in the infamous 1979 court case, continues her legal battle to reopen the process and clear her name.
